prompt:
Our workflow tooling reads small YAML config documents, and I am done dragging a YAML dependency into every little CLI that touches them. The files we emit only ever use a well-behaved block-style slice of YAML, so I want our own loader: yamlite.ts, stdlib only, runs under node 26 with type stripping (so: no enums, no namespaces, no decorators, no parameter properties, and imports must spell out the ./file.ts extension).

Exports:
- parse(text: string): unknown — returns plain JS values (null, boolean, number, string, arrays, plain objects).
- class YamlParseError extends Error with 1-based line and col properties; every message starts with "line L, col C: " followed by the detail.

The subset, exactly:
- Lines split on \n; a trailing \r is stripped (CRLF files come from the Windows boxes). Indentation is SPACES ONLY — a tab anywhere in leading whitespace is an error at the tab ("tab character in indentation").
- Comments: a # at the start of the line's content, or preceded by whitespace, starts a comment (never inside quotes; nothing is a comment inside block scalars). a#b is all scalar. Blank and comment-only lines are skipped. An empty document (or only comments) parses to null.
- Block mappings: "key: value" or "key:" (colon at end of line) for nested/empty values. The separator is a colon followed by a space or end of line — url: http://h:8080/x is one scalar value. Keys are plain text up to the separator, or quoted strings; keys are ALWAYS strings, used verbatim (1: x gives key "1"). A duplicate key in the same mapping is an error at the duplicate key (duplicate mapping key "name"). A key with nothing after the colon takes the following more-indented block as its value; if the next content line is a sequence entry at the SAME indent as the key, that sequence is the value (both indented and same-indent sequence styles are common in our files); otherwise the value is null.
- Block sequences: "- item" entries, one per line, all at the block's indent. The indicator is a dash followed by a space or end of line (- -5 is the number -5). A dash with nothing after it takes a following more-indented block as the item, else null. The rest of the line after "- " is a node in its own right starting at that column: "- name: a" opens a compact mapping whose further entries sit at that same column on the following lines, and "- - a" starts a nested sequence the same way. A non-dash line at the indent of a sequence block is an error ("expected '-' sequence indicator") — except when the sequence sits at its parent mapping's indent, where a mapping entry hands control back to the mapping.
- Nesting is by indentation. Every content line must line up with an open block: a line indented deeper than expected, or a dedent that lands between levels, is an error at its first content character ("unexpected indentation"). A second content line after a single-scalar document (or after a scalar value's line, same thing) is "unexpected content after scalar". Plain scalars are single-line — there is no multi-line plain folding in this subset.
- Plain scalars are trimmed and typed: exactly lowercase true/false are booleans; exactly lowercase null, ~, or empty is null; integer text (optional sign, digits) and float text (optional sign, digits with a decimal point and/or e/E exponent) are numbers. EVERYTHING else is a string — True, FALSE, Null, yes, no, on stay strings (we got bitten by the Norway problem once; never again). A plain VALUE containing a colon-space is an error at that colon ("unexpected ':' in plain scalar") — compact mappings only open after a dash, never after key:.
- Double-quoted scalars: escapes \" \\ \/ \n \t \r and \uXXXX (4 hex digits). Anything else after a backslash is "invalid escape sequence \c" at the backslash; a malformed \u is "invalid unicode escape" at the backslash. Single-quoted scalars: '' is a literal quote, backslashes are literal. Quoted scalars are never typed ("true" is a string). An unclosed quote is "unterminated double-quoted string"/"unterminated single-quoted string" at the OPENING quote (quoted scalars do not span lines). After the closing quote only whitespace/comment may follow, else "unexpected content after quoted scalar" at the offending character.
- Block scalars as values or sequence items: | (literal) and > (folded), optionally with a - chomp modifier (|- >-). After the indicator and optional -, only whitespace/comment may follow — anything else (including digits or +) is "unexpected content after block scalar indicator" at that character. Content lines must be indented deeper than the column of the entry that introduces the scalar (the key's column, or the dash's column for a plain sequence item); the first content line fixes the block indent, that many leading spaces are stripped from every line, deeper indentation is preserved as text. Blank lines are allowed inside. A non-blank line indented less than the block indent but still deeper than the owner ends nothing cleanly — that is "bad indentation in block scalar" at its first character. The block ends at the first non-blank line at or left of the owner column. Literal joins lines with \n; folded joins consecutive non-blank lines with single spaces and turns each run of blank lines into one \n. Default chomping clips to exactly one trailing newline (empty content stays ""); the - modifier strips trailing newlines entirely.
- Explicitly OUT of scope, and rejected with errors at the offending character: flow collections — a value starting with [ or { is "flow collections are not supported"; anchors, aliases, and tags — a value starting with &, *, or ! is "anchors, aliases, and tags are not supported". (Mid-text & and * are ordinary characters.) Multi-document streams (---) never occur in our files.

test_yamlite.ts is the acceptance contract — node --test test_yamlite.ts must pass, and don't edit the tests. Please keep the parser a single file.
